findings.finding: Pre-fix bug pin test (TestConvergenceMediumPath test 6) is tautological: evaluate_convergence() injects reviewer_json_missing for ANY missing expected role unconditionally. A revert of Medium rows in reviewer-convergence.md would NOT be caught.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with row-scoped content assertion parsing Medium Stage 3 row and asserting threat-modeler absent from that row's roles cell.

findings.finding: Test 4 (superset-of-Deep) is vacuous: evaluate_convergence iterates expected_roles, not reviewer_jsons.keys(). Extra keys in jsons are always silently ignored.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with actual regression guard: expected_roles=[code-reviewer, phase-auditor], jsons only those two → converged=True, meta_critical_count=0.

findings.finding: No test pin for extra-reviewer-in-jsons-is-ignored behavior. Refactor of evaluate_convergence to iterate jsons.keys() would cause spurious CRITICALs undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add test_extra_reviewer_in_jsons_is_ignored asserting tester not in findings_by_role when expected_roles=[code-reviewer, phase-auditor].

findings.finding: Global substring searches for absence assertions (e.g. threat-modeler not in content) would pass even if threat-modeler only appears in Deep rows.
findings.suggested_fix: Use row-scoped regex _medium_row() to extract roles cell of the specific Medium row before asserting absence.

findings.finding: Empty expected_roles edge case not tested.
